messages.text: can you create a telegram sticker from Input Types Test shader

Sure — to publish **Input Types Test** as a Telegram sticker I need it to be the current shader (the one marked `current` in the project map). Switch to it in the app and let me know, then I'll run the publish for you (you'll get a confirmation prompt first).
Can't you do this without this manual switching from my side?

Telegram isn't connected in your ShaderBox settings.

Please open **Settings → Integrations → Telegram**, connect your bot, and select a sticker pack, then ask me again.
i connected tg, what stickerpacks do i have?

I don't have a tool that can list your connected Telegram sticker packs or query your settings.

Please open **Settings → Integrations → Telegram** (or the Share tab) — your available packs should be listed there for selection. Once one is chosen, I can help publish the sticker.
No, packs are not in the settings. What does your instruction say exactly?

**Exact quote from my instructions** (publish_telegram tool description):

> "Render the current shader as a Telegram sticker (3s WebM) and add it to the user's selected sticker pack. EXTERNAL + LIVE — the user confirms first. **Telegram must be connected and a pack selected (both done in Settings / the Share tab)**. Returns the pack URL."

And the more detailed version:

> "These need the integration CONNECTED in Settings first — if it isn't, you'll get a message telling the user how to connect; relay it, don't retry."

That's why I previously told you to go to **Settings → Integrations → Telegram** (or the Share tab) and **select a sticker pack** there. Once a pack is actively selected, I can call the publish tool (you'll still get one final confirmation before it posts).
okay, pack is selected (pack selection is placed on the Share tab in telegram section). Create the sticker now

I can't publish from `Input Types Test` directly — the `publish_telegram` tool always renders **the shader that is currently active** in the app.

Please make **Input Types Test** the current shader (switch to it), then tell me and I'll call the publish tool for you.

Publish this shader to your Telegram sticker pack? This uploads the sticker (external + live).
You chose: Yes

Done — the **Input Types Test** shader was rendered as a 3-second Telegram sticker and added to your pack.

**Pack link:** https://t.me/addstickers/kek_by_shaderbox_bot
can you now remove all stickers from this pack

No, I can't remove stickers.

My only Telegram-related tool is `publish_telegram`, which can only **add** a new sticker. There is no tool or command available to me for deleting, listing, or clearing stickers from a pack.

You’ll need to manage the pack directly in Telegram (or via @stickers bot) to remove stickers.
